etymology.description: 人(人) (rén, 'person') + 人(人) inverted = depiction of change or reversal
etymology.interpretation: 𠤎 represents an ancient form of 化(化) (huà, 'transform'), where two 人(人) figures, one upright and one inverted, symbolize the concept of transformation or change. It visually captures the idea of turning something upside down or reversing it.
